Thomas Summons of Tacoma, pri-
rived in Spokane this morning from
Walla Walla, where he left President
RoOsevelts train. Mr.. Sammons has
been on the presidents train since 1t
left Tacoma. He said:


president Roosevelt expressed
himself as expecting to see some re-
markable things in Washington, but
that the state had surpassed his ex-
pectations. The president spoke of
our humber and fishing industries on
the coast and was greatly impressed
with the agricultural wealth of the
east and southeast.


During our ride across the state
the president had much to say about
irrigation and his vi can only mean
advancement in that line to the state.
His train is running on time and he
will likely reach Spokane as per
schedule"


Mr. Summons stated that after
each stop the messengers on the
presidents special go through the
train and check up on the party and
IL anybody Is missing a search is at
once instituted. At Tacoma New
York Sun reporter missed the train
and he was compelled to charter an
engine and make a mile a minute run
to catch up.
